summary: Iteration 2 testing: Validated all 5 bug fixes from main agent. Backend: 15/15 tests passing, no warnings on startup (no IntakeResponse warnings). Frontend: Home screen StatBox stats grid renders correctly, Track screen loads with analytics data in single pass (no double-call observed), Today screen renders with FINISH SESSION button visible, Review screen at /review?setsLogged=16&totalSets=20&sessionType=Max%20Effort%20Upper&week=2 renders all sections (hero, stats, wins, coach note, next steps, CTA buttons). All features working as expected.
action_items: LOW: The 404 error seen in browser console is for /api/plan/session/today (program router endpoint not implemented in this version - not a regression, was pre-existing). Normal behavior.
INFO: MOCKED - today.tsx EXERCISES constant is still mock data. Real exercises not yet pulled from backend.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: Route /api/prs/bests/overview defined AFTER /api/prs/{exercise} - but since FastAPI matches bests as exercise param ONLY when bests is NOT matched first, this could be a problem. Recommend reordering to put /prs/bests/overview BEFORE /prs/{exercise}. (Currently working but fragile)
api.ts: Uses EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL - confirmed correct env var name
seed_data_creation: Profile injected to localStorage for testing. Backend seed data was already present from previous iteration.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The app requires localStorage 'profile' key to be set (key = 'profile' per storage.ts KEYS.PROFILE). Without it, all tab screens show 'No profile found'. Set up via onboarding or localStorage injection. Backend analyticsApi and substitutionApi both confirmed working (200 OK). Review screen requires URL params: setsLogged, totalSets, sessionType, week. Track screen analytics load from /api/analytics/* endpoints (all 200 OK). The 404 for /api/plan/session/today is expected - that endpoint is from program router which is not fully implemented.
rca_of_issue: N/A - All tested features are working correctly. Previous iteration's onboarding navigation bug (router.replace from step6) is pre-existing and unrelated to this iteration's changes.
